_description: H2Dev Channel Knowledge Base — Extracted from ancient_humans_master_prompt.md. Injected into all AI prompts for consistent quality.

content_dna.niche: Ancient humans, human prehistory, evolution, anthropology, and survival — how early humans actually lived, hunted, slept, raised children, and handled everyday life. Occasional crossovers into medieval life, weird history, and human psychology.
content_dna.format: 7–12 minute educational explainer narrated in calm, intelligent 2nd-person ('you', 'your ancestors', 'your body', 'your brain') — never 'we' or 'I'. Tone is smart but playful — weave in dry humor and witty observations to keep the viewer entertained.
content_dna.hook_formula: Open by dropping the viewer *inside* an ancestral or everyday sensory moment in 2nd person ('You wake up when your body is ready. No alarm, no schedule.') → immediately pivot with a reframe ('For 99% of human history, this wasn't a hypothetical.' / 'For roughly 300,000 years...') → contrast it against a striking modern statistic that reframes everything.
content_dna.script_rhythm: Short sentence. Short sentence. One longer sentence that builds depth. Short sentence. Question? — repeat this pattern throughout.
narrative_arc: Reframe — a stark contrast or modern statistic that pivots the topic
Evidence stack — a named study → cross-cultural / skeletal / archaeological confirmation
Reconstruct — a concrete scene the viewer can picture ('So let's reconstruct a day...')
Counterintuitive twist — e.g., 'agriculture was a trap'
Modern mirror — reflecting the truth onto the viewer's current life
Echo closing — ending line that directly echoes the very first line, completely reframed
content_dna.evidence_rule: Weave at least 3 real named researchers, studies, or archaeological sites naturally into the narration (e.g., Richard Lee's 1963 study, James Suzman, Polly Wiessner, Chauvet Cave, Blombos Cave). Never invent names — only use real ones.
content_dna.jargon_rule: No jargon without plain-English explanation — every scientific or anthropological term gets decoded immediately.
content_dna.humor_rule: Weave in dry, deadpan humor throughout — the kind that makes viewers smile mid-sentence without breaking the educational tone. Techniques: (1) Absurd modern comparisons ('Your ancestors didn't have alarm clocks. They also didn't have existential dread about Monday mornings. Coincidence?'), (2) Understated sarcasm about modern life ('You spend 8 hours a day under fluorescent lights staring at spreadsheets. Your ancestors would call that a hostage situation.'), (3) Unexpected punchlines after a serious buildup ('They survived ice ages, predator attacks, and starvation. You get stressed when the Wi-Fi drops.'), (4) Anthropomorphizing ancient problems with modern slang ('Neanderthals were basically the quiet kid in class who turned out to be a genius.'). Never force a joke — humor must flow naturally from the facts. Never use corny puns or exclamation marks for jokes. The funniest lines are the ones delivered completely straight.
content_dna.ending_rule: Always ends by reflecting the ancient or scientific truth back onto something the viewer feels or does today.

viral_topic_angles.description: Bridges a universal modern concern (jobs, privacy, sleep, raising children, hygiene) to prehistoric life.
Survival against a vivid threat (deadliest predators, deadly winters, the Ice Age, starvation).

viral_topic_angles.template: The CRAZIEST / WEIRDEST ___ Used by Ancient Humans
viral_topic_angles.description: A superlative, curiosity-gap reveal of strange real methods.

viral_topic_angles.template: What ___ Was Like in Ancient / Medieval Times
viral_topic_angles.description: An everyday or taboo bodily/social topic (the bathroom, periods, dating, hygiene, unwanted pregnancy) treated honestly.

viral_topic_angles.template: Why You Wouldn't Last a Day in ___ / POV: Your Life as ___
viral_topic_angles.description: An immersive 2nd-person scenario that puts the viewer in the past.
A provocative existential or counterfactual question grounded in real data (e.g., 'What If Neanderthals Had Not Gone Extinct?').

visual_style_dna.art_style: Hand-drawn 2D doodle cartoon animation — flat solid colors, bold black hand-drawn outlines, slightly wobbly/imperfect lines as if sketched fast with a marker. Childlike and simple. Minimal interior line detail allowed (wood grain, rock cracks) but always flat — ZERO gradients, ZERO drop shadows, ZERO photographic textures.
visual_style_dna.style_anchor: Hand-drawn 2D doodle cartoon animation, flat solid colors, bold black hand-drawn outlines, slightly wobbly imperfect marker lines,
visual_style_dna.style_lock: no gradients, no drop shadows, no photographic textures, no photorealism, no 3D render, no realistic faces, no anime, 16:9 widescreen, simple educational YouTube explainer doodle style.

main_character.description: A stick figure with a large round white head, simple black dot eyes, expressive thin eyebrows, a simple line mouth, and thin black stick limbs. The signature recurring character has spiky bright ORANGE hair. Hands are small black blobs/mittens.

character_variants.you_main: spiky bright ORANGE hair — the main 'you' character
character_variants.modern_neutral: bald round white head (no hair) — neutral modern everyman
character_variants.elder_grandmother: gray hair in a bun + colored dress — elder/grandmother

visual_style_dna.animals_objects: Chunky, simplified cartoon shapes — big, bold, flat single-color fills with thick black outlines (wooden crates, gray boulders, lanterns, cribs, campfires, simple trees, shields).

neutral_modern.colors: White or cream top + gray ground strip

outdoor_daytime.colors: Light blue sky + tan/brown ground + green trees

ancient_savanna.colors: Orange sky + tan ground + grass tufts + lone acacia tree
ancient_savanna.use_for: ancient / prehistoric savanna (dawn, dusk, 'deep past' tone)

calm_night.colors: Dark navy blue + yellow crescent moon + gray ground

deep_night.colors: Deep indigo/purple + scattered star dots + brown ground

visual_style_dna.on_screen_text: Bold hand-lettered marker font, large, usually centered or upper-center. Default color RED; use WHITE when the text sits on a dark object (e.g., a label written across a gray boulder). Numbers and key concepts get their own full text frame.

proven_frame_types.concept_text: Plain white/cream background + bold red ALL-CAPS hand-lettered text centered (a number, a term, a key phrase).
proven_frame_types.label_on_object: A large object (boulder, hourglass) with the key word hand-lettered across it in white or red caps.
proven_frame_types.thought_bubble: Cloud-shaped bubble above a stick figure's head containing a mini-scene or symbol ('?', 'HMMM').
proven_frame_types.red_x_negation: A confident figure or idea with a big bold red X drawn across the whole frame = 'not this / wrong'.
proven_frame_types.tribe_campfire: Several white-headed stick figures around an orange campfire on tan ground under a light blue sky.
proven_frame_types.archaeologist_discovery: A white-headed stick figure with a brown pith helmet, backpack, and yellow lantern beside a dark cave entrance in brown rock, light blue sky.
proven_frame_types.evolution_sequence: Left-to-right human/creature progression with a black right-pointing arrow.
proven_frame_types.sadness_hardship: A sad orange-haired stick figure with arms hugging knees under a dark gray rain cloud with blue raindrops.

seo_dna.title_rules: One scroll-stopping, curiosity-driven title under 70 characters. Use the channel's proven angles ('What/How Did Ancient Humans ___?', a survival hook, a superlative reveal, or an immersive POV). No clickbait the script doesn't deliver on.
description_structure: Open with a 2–3 sentence hook that mirrors the tone of the script's opening and teases the core reframe.
Follow with a short paragraph (3–4 sentences) summarizing what the viewer will discover, written in the calm 2nd-person voice.
Add a line inviting likes, comments, and subscribes in the channel's voice.
End with a block of 15–25 relevant hashtags on one line (each starting with #).
seo_dna.tags_rules: 25–40 SEO tags in a single comma-separated line. Mix broad terms (ancient humans, human evolution, prehistory, anthropology, early humans, hunter gatherers) with specific long-tail phrases pulled from the video's topic. No hashtags here — plain comma-separated keywords only.

image_prompt_rules.abstract_to_concrete: Translate abstract narration into concrete visuals — if the script says 'survival was a constant struggle', show a worried orange-haired stick figure straining to push a huge dark gray boulder with bold white ALL-CAPS text 'SURVIVAL' on it; if it says '300,000 years', show a plain white background with bold red hand-lettered text '300,000 YEARS' centered.
image_prompt_rules.scene_holding: Hold scenes across consecutive timestamps — if 3 lines describe the same moment, keep the same scene and only adjust the character's expression or add one new element. Do not generate a brand-new scene every 5 seconds.
image_prompt_rules.character_consistency: Keep the recurring character consistent: the main 'you' figure is a round white-headed stick figure with spiky orange hair; ancient humans have messy brown hair; neutral modern figures have a bald white head. State the hair/head explicitly in every prompt that includes a person.

background_tone_matching.ancient_prehistoric: orange sky + tan ground + grass + lone acacia tree
background_tone_matching.outdoor_nature: light blue sky + tan-brown ground + simple green trees
background_tone_matching.calm_night: dark navy background + yellow crescent moon + gray ground
background_tone_matching.deep_night_sleeping: deep indigo background + scattered star dots + brown ground
background_tone_matching.neutral_modern_concept: white or cream background (+ gray ground strip if a floor is needed)
background_tone_matching.danger_hardship_sadness: white or cream background with a dark rain cloud, blue raindrops, or bold red text
